quotes.quote: The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.
quotes.rationale: Identifies an LLM evaluator whose output ranks candidates on stated quality criteria, selecting which candidates advance.

quotes.quote: When comparing the LLM judge’s preferences with experts’, the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10 (Supplementary Figure S11A). This concordance is more than double that expected from random selection. Furthermore, the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts (Supplementary Figure S11B). When presented with identical pairwise comparisons, the LLM judge selected the same hypothesis in 88% of comparisons, as compared with human experts who selected the same hypothesis 61% of the time.
quotes.rationale: Reported reliability evidence for this exact channel is expert-agreement and intra-rater consistency, i.e. an agreement study, not calibration or ground-truth validation.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.
quotes.rationale: Physical experiments are executed by human researchers, not the system; the resulting measurements are the empirical evidence channel.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: Shows the experimental result is routed back into generating a new round of candidates, i.e. candidate revision rather than terminal assessment.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Finch is an internal automated analysis component acting on human-preprocessed sequencing data; only the downstream differential expression step is automated.

quotes.quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis. Robin then interprets the results of this analysis to generate a new round of therapeutic candidates.
quotes.rationale: The analysis output feeds interpretation that produces the next round of candidates.

quotes.quote: Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.
quotes.rationale: A distinct human-substrate analysis channel for a dataset not analyzed by the automated component; downstream routing of this human analysis is not stated.

quotes.quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
quotes.rationale: Confirms human performance of upstream sequencing preprocessing within the same reported workflow.

quotes.quote: For example, while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols—future iterations aim to provide detailed methodologies that require minimal human translation for laboratory execution.
quotes.rationale: The source states outputs are not executable protocols and that improvement is a future aim; no executability check is exercised.

policy_type: LLM-judged tournament ranking of candidates on scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and supporting-literature methodology (R10-E03)
human-in-the-loop iteration: humans run experiments and return data, system interprets and generates the next candidate round (R10-E02, R10-E05)

diagnostics.statement: The only reliability evidence for the ranking channel is concordance with expert preferences and intra-rater repeatability; no ground-truth or outcome-based validation of ranking correctness is reported.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: the LLM judge demonstrated high concordance with expert preferences, with an average of 7.25 of its top 10 hypotheses matching those in the experts’ top 10
The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament according to the strength of the scientific rationale, pharmacological profile, and methodology of the supporting literature.

diagnostics.consequence: Agreement with experts cannot establish that the ranking selects candidates that succeed experimentally, so the gate controlling which candidates advance rests on unvalidated proxy quality.

diagnostics.statement: The system does not produce executable laboratory protocols and physical experiments are conducted by human researchers, so the loop from candidate to physical evidence depends on human translation and execution.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: while Robin generates experimental outlines, it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols
Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.

diagnostics.consequence: Reported autonomy claims cover ideation and analysis but not execution; the boundary of the automated system must be stated explicitly to avoid overreading closure.

diagnostics.statement: Key analysis steps in the reported workflow were performed by humans rather than the automated analysis component, and no independent replication of the experimental findings is reported.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human and subsequent differential gene expression analysis was performed by Finch.
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

diagnostics.consequence: The division of analytic labor makes it unclear how much of the reported empirical validation is attributable to the automated validator channel.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: the LLM judge exhibited higher intra-rater consistency than human experts
The drug candidates are ranked by an LLM-judged tournament

success_check.observable: Experimental outcome rate of candidates ranked in the judge's top tier versus lower-ranked or randomly drawn candidates.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Top-ranked candidates show a statistically significant higher assay hit rate than a matched lower-ranked comparator set.
success_check.evidence_needed: Prospective physical assay results reported per rank stratum with the pre-specified comparator.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: it does not yet produce precise, executable protocols
Read demultiplexing and alignment was performed by a human
Flow cytometry data was also analyzed by a human scientist.

success_check.observable: A per-step attribution table stating, for every generation, execution, and analysis step, whether the actor was the automated component or a human.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every reported workflow step has an explicit actor label with no unattributed steps.
success_check.evidence_needed: Published methods table or figure with step-level actor attribution.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Researchers next conduct the experiments and provide the resulting data to Robin for autonomous analysis.

success_check.observable: Repetition of the reported assays by a laboratory not involved in the original runs.
success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Direction and significance of the key reported effect reproduced in the independent replication.
success_check.evidence_needed: Replication report with raw measurements and analysis code from the separate laboratory.
